wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ForwarderKE project overview section focused on the ESP32-based CAN bus control system for agricultural forwarder machines. Explain the project's purpose as an open-source replacement for factory controllers, its J1939-like CAN protocol implementation, and the three ECU architecture (Motor Driver, Joystick 1, Joystick 2). Document the system's target applications in agricultural machinery, hardware requirements including LilyGO T-CAN boards and PCA9685 PWM controllers, and the overall system architecture. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to embedded CAN systems and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'ECU', 'address claiming', and 'J1939-like'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the three-ECU system operation and explain the benefits of open-source control systems for agricultural equipment.

wiki_catalogs.name: MCU Board Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ESP32-S3 LilyGO T-CAN board setup and configuration. Document the board specifications including built-in CAN transceiver capabilities, memory allocation, and power requirements. Detail the default pin assignments for CAN TX/RX signals, onboard WS2812 status LED, and I2C connections. Explain the PlatformIO build environments and how to select the appropriate ECU type during compilation. Include step-by-step flashing procedures for different ECU variants (motor driver, joystick 1, joystick 2). Document the OTA build process and wireless update capabilities. Address power supply considerations, voltage regulation, and current limitations for the ESP32-S3 processor.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build System Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build system documentation for ForwarderKE's PlatformIO configuration. Document the six distinct build environments and their purposes, explaining how each environment targets different ECU types and deployment scenarios. Detail the compiler flags, build options, and platform-specific settings for ESP32 development. Explain the environment variable system used for ECU type selection (ECU_TYPE_MOTOR_DRIVER vs ECU_TYPE_JOYSTICK). Document the release management process, dependency resolution, and cross-compilation setup. Include practical examples of building for different environments and troubleshooting common build issues. Address the CI/CD pipeline integration with GitHub Actions and automated deployment workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Addressing and Arbitration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the CAN addressing and arbitration system in ForwarderKE. Explain the J1939-like 29-bit extended ID format with detailed breakdown of priority (bits 28-26), data page (bit 24), parameter group number (bits 23-16), and source addressing (bits 7-0). Document the address claiming mechanism that runs during system startup to prevent conflicts between multiple ECUs. Detail the arbitration states including ACS_CLAIMING, ACS_CLAIMED, ACS_CANNOT_CLAIM, and ACS_WAIT_RETRY. Explain the timing parameters including CLAIM_TIMEOUT_MS (250ms), CLAIM_RETRY_MS (1000ms), and MAX_CLAIM_ATTEMPTS (5). Document the special addresses SA_CANNOT_CLAIM (0xFE) and SA_BROADCAST (0xFF). Include sequence diagrams showing the address claiming process, conflict resolution procedures, and retry mechanisms. Provide practical examples of address assignment scenarios and troubleshooting common arbitration failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Motor Driver ECU
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Motor Driver ECU implementation focused on solenoid control and PCA9685 PWM management. Document the PCA9685 initialization sequence, I2C configuration, and dual-controller support for up to 16 channels. Explain the PWM mapping algorithm that converts joystick values to solenoid actuation signals, including bidirectional axis handling and deadband calculations. Detail the safety timeout mechanism that automatically shuts off solenoids after 500ms of inactivity. Document the LED status indicator system showing operational states, connection status, and identification modes. Explain the heartbeat message generation containing system health metrics and CAN bus statistics. Cover the address claiming process, configuration persistence, and CAN message processing for joystick data, solenoid commands, and LED control. Include practical examples of axis configuration, PWM frequency settings, and troubleshooting procedures for common issues like solenoid sticking or communication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the configuration data models used in ForwarderKE's persistent storage system. Detail the AxisConfig structure for joystick-to-solenoid mapping including sourceAddress, potIndex, outputChannel, deadband settings, PWM ranges, and flags. Document the CanOutputRule structure for GPIO control rules including match criteria, output pin configuration, mode settings, and timing parameters. Explain the MotorConfig structure that aggregates multiple AxisConfig instances and PCA controller management. Cover the packed data format for CAN transport efficiency, bit field packing for compact storage, and serialization/deserialization methods. Include field definitions, value ranges, default values, and the relationship between configuration structures. Provide examples of configuration object initialization, modification, and validation procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Server Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the web server architecture focused on the ESP32 HTTP server implementation using the WebServer library. Document the server initialization process, WiFi access point creation, mDNS service advertisement, and port 80 HTTP endpoint configuration. Explain the modular server design with separate handler functions for different routes (/, /api/state, /api/config, /api/canoutput, /update). Detail the static HTML page serving mechanism, JavaScript-driven real-time updates, and WebSocket-like polling system using setInterval. Cover the server lifecycle management, client request handling, and response formatting. Include practical examples of server startup sequences, route registration, and client-server communication patterns. Address server security considerations, access point credentials, and network connectivity requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTA System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive OTA system architecture documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the wireless firmware update infrastructure and deployment mechanisms. Document the WiFi access point creation process during OTA mode, including SSID configuration, default password setup, and network isolation strategies. Explain the web server implementation using ESP32's built-in HTTP server capabilities, including endpoint routing, static file serving, and dynamic content generation. Detail the mDNS service discovery setup for automatic hostname resolution and network connectivity. Document the integration between OTA functionality and the broader CAN ecosystem, including heartbeat scanning and module detection during updates. Address the conditional compilation aspects using ENABLE_OTA_WEBSERVER flag and its impact on build configurations. Include practical examples of OTA startup sequences, network configuration procedures, and system state management during firmware updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP REST API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive HTTP REST API documentation for ForwarderKE web interface endpoints. Document all GET/POST endpoints including /api/state for real-time system status, /api/config for axis mapping configuration, /api/identify for device identification, /api/address for address assignment, /api/canoutput for GPIO rule configuration, and /update for OTA firmware updates. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas with complete JSON structures, parameter validation rules, and expected status codes. Include practical curl command examples, JavaScript fetch API usage patterns, and error handling scenarios. Document authentication requirements, rate limiting considerations, and CORS policies. Provide detailed examples of request payloads and response formats with actual data structures from the codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Diagnostic Procedures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive diagnostic procedures documentation for ForwarderKE focused on systematic troubleshooting methodologies and diagnostic tools. Document CAN bus communication diagnostics using oscilloscope measurements, serial debug output interpretation, and heartbeat monitoring analysis. Explain how to use the built-in diagnostic capabilities including error counters, status reporting, and system health indicators. Detail the diagnostic workflow for identifying hardware faults, software bugs, and configuration issues. Include step-by-step procedures for measuring CAN bus signal integrity, analyzing message traffic patterns, and interpreting system status messages. Document the use of serial monitors for real-time debugging, log analysis techniques, and performance monitoring procedures. Provide diagnostic checklists for common failure scenarios and systematic approaches to isolate problems in multi-ECU CAN bus systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Protocol Extensions and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for protocol extensions and customization in ForwarderKE. Document the process of extending the J1939-like CAN protocol by defining new parameter group numbers (PGNs), message formats, and addressing schemes. Explain how to implement custom message types beyond the existing joystick, LED, solenoid, and configuration messages. Detail the protocol definition structure including priority bits, data page handling, PDU format specification, and destination addressing. Provide step-by-step guidance for adding new CAN message handlers, implementing custom ECU types, and extending the address claiming mechanism. Include examples of protocol enhancement scenarios such as adding support for additional sensors, actuators, or control signals. Document the relationship between PF values, PS addressing, and message routing. Address backward compatibility considerations when extending the protocol and provide migration strategies for existing deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analog Input Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the analog input processing subsystem of the Joystick ECU. Document the ADC configuration, sampling rates, and resolution settings for joystick potentiometer inputs. Explain the analog-to-digital conversion process, including reference voltage selection and calibration procedures. Detail the input filtering techniques used to reduce noise and improve signal stability. Cover the deadband configuration for zero-center joystick positioning and sensitivity adjustment parameters. Document the raw ADC value ranges, scaling factors, and coordinate transformation algorithms. Include practical examples of ADC calibration, noise reduction filtering, and input validation procedures. Address common issues with analog signal quality and troubleshooting techniques for unstable readings.

wiki_catalogs.name: PCA9685 PWM Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PCA9685 PWM control subsystem. Document the dual-controller architecture supporting up to 16 channels with automatic detection of secondary PCA9685 controller. Explain the I2C initialization sequence, oscillator frequency configuration (25MHz), and PWM frequency settings (200Hz). Detail the channel mapping logic that routes solenoid outputs to specific PCA9685 channels, including the setPWM function implementation and boundary checking. Cover the initialization routine that configures both PCA9685 controllers, wire pin assignments, and address detection. Include practical examples of channel allocation, PWM value conversion from 12-bit to 16-bit resolution, and troubleshooting procedures for I2C communication failures or channel conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Status Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for system status API endpoints including /api/state for real-time system monitoring and /api/modules for device discovery. Document the GET /api/state endpoint which returns comprehensive system health data including local address, CAN bus connectivity status, joystick input readings, solenoid output values, and detected modules. Specify the complete JSON response schema with all fields (localAddr, online, uptime, txCount, rxCount, errCount, joy array with potentiometer values and button states, sol array with solenoid values, and modules array with address, type, uptime, and age). Include practical curl examples showing how to retrieve and parse the JSON response. Document the /api/modules endpoint for discovering connected CAN devices and their properties.

wiki_catalogs.name: Axis Mapping Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for axis mapping configuration endpoints. Document the GET /api/config endpoint for retrieving current axis mapping configuration including pcaCount and axes array with complete field definitions (sourceAddress, potIndex, outputChannel, deadbandMin, deadbandMax, pwmMin, pwmMax, flags). Document the POST /api/config endpoint for saving new axis mappings with the request payload structure containing axisIdx, sourceAddress, potIndex, outputChannel, deadbandMin, deadbandMax, pwmMin, pwmMax, and flags for each axis. Include practical examples of complete configuration payloads, validation rules for each parameter, and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for ForwarderKE focused on initial setup and first-time usage. Document prerequisites including PlatformIO installation, ESP32-S3 development environment setup, and hardware preparation. Provide step-by-step hardware assembly instructions for LilyGO T-CAN boards, PCA9685 motor driver connections, and joystick wiring. Include detailed build and flashing procedures using PlatformIO environments for motor driver, joystick1, and joystick2 configurations. Document the address claiming process during initial startup and explain the compile-time ECU type selection mechanism. Cover WiFi access point setup for OTA updates, web interface access, and basic troubleshooting for common setup issues. Include safety guidelines for working with CAN bus systems and electrical components. Provide practical examples of successful setup verification and initial testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Transceiver Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the CAN bus implementation using the built-in transceiver on the LilyGO T-CAN board. Explain the J1939-like protocol implementation with 29-bit extended IDs and the specific addressing scheme (0x20 for motor driver, 0x21 and 0x22 for joysticks). Document the CAN message structure including priority bits, data page, parameter group numbers, and source addresses. Detail the address claiming mechanism for collision avoidance and the heartbeat broadcast system. Include CAN bus topology requirements, termination resistors, and signal integrity considerations. Explain the TWAI (Tightly-coupled Wireless Automotive) controller configuration and error handling mechanisms. Address bus-off recovery and automatic reconnection procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: ECU Type Selection System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ForwarderKE's ECU type selection mechanism using preprocessor directives and compile-time configuration. Explain how the main.cpp file uses conditional compilation to select between motor driver and joystick ECU implementations. Document the ECU_TYPE_MOTOR_DRIVER and ECU_TYPE_JOYSTICK build flags, their effects on included headers, and runtime behavior differences. Detail the factory pattern implementation where different ECU types share common interfaces but have distinct functionality. Include practical examples of switching between ECU types during development and deployment. Address the benefits of compile-time selection for memory optimization and performance tuning.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Formats and Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for CAN message formats and types in ForwarderKE. Document all custom PF (Parameter Group Number) definitions including PF_JOYSTICK_POT1 (0x10), PF_JOYSTICK_POT2 (0x11), PF_JOYSTICK_POT3 (0x12), PF_JOYSTICK_BUTTONS (0x13), PF_LED_COLOR (0x20), PF_SOLENOID_CMD (0x21), PF_IDENTIFY (0x22), PF_SET_ADDRESS (0x23), PF_CONFIG_AXIS (0x24), PF_REQUEST_CONFIG (0x25), PF_CONFIG_RESPONSE (0x26), and PF_HEARTBEAT (0x30). Explain the payload structure for each message type, data encoding schemes, and length specifications. Detail the difference between broadcast (DA_BROADCAST = 0xFF) and directed messages where PS field becomes destination address. Document the CANMessage structure with id, data[8], len, and ext fields. Include practical examples of message construction, payload formatting, and interpretation using the actual codebase implementation. Explain the relationship between PF values and their corresponding functionality in the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Joystick ECU
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Joystick ECU implementation focused on analog input processing and CAN message broadcasting. Document the analog-to-digital conversion process, sampling rates, and input filtering techniques used to ensure stable joystick readings. Explain the button state management system including debouncing algorithms and state change detection. Detail the CAN message formatting for joystick potentiometer data, including the PF_JOYSTICK_POT1/2/3 message types and data encoding. Document the input calibration procedures, deadband configuration, and sensitivity adjustments. Explain the real-time data transmission to motor driver ECUs and the heartbeat mechanism for maintaining connection status. Cover the interrupt-driven input handling, timing constraints, and memory management for smooth operation. Include practical examples of joystick calibration, CAN message monitoring, and troubleshooting input responsiveness issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: NVS Storage System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Non-Volatile Storage (NVS) implementation in ForwarderKE's configuration management. Explain the Preferences library integration, namespace organization ('fwdrcfg'), and key-value storage mechanisms. Document the configuration persistence workflow including load/save operations, data serialization formats, and storage location management. Detail the address override functionality for forced device addressing, motor configuration storage, and CAN output rule persistence. Cover storage capacity limits, write endurance considerations, and flash memory management. Include error handling for storage failures, corruption detection, and recovery procedures. Provide examples of configuration loading sequences, storage validation checks, and troubleshooting storage-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard and Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the real-time dashboard and monitoring system focused on the interactive web interface displaying system status and device telemetry. Document the main dashboard layout with joystick monitoring panels, solenoid output visualization, and CAN bus statistics display. Explain the real-time data refresh mechanism using JavaScript polling every 200ms, JSON API integration for live updates, and dynamic UI rendering with SVG-based progress bars. Detail the joystick input visualization showing potentiometer readings and button states, solenoid output monitoring with channel-by-channel PWM value displays, and CAN bus metrics including TX/RX counts and error rates. Cover the module detection system showing discovered devices with type identification, uptime tracking, and last-seen timestamps. Include practical examples of dashboard customization, data interpretation, and troubleshooting display issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Interface and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the OTA web interface and security implementation in ForwarderKE. Document the HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend architecture including the tabbed interface design, real-time dashboard components, and interactive controls for firmware updates. Explain the security model including the default WiFi password 'forwarder123', network isolation through access point mode, and the limited attack surface of the embedded web server. Detail the JavaScript client-side implementation including AJAX polling for real-time data, form validation, progress tracking, and error handling. Document the security considerations for field deployments including network segmentation, credential management, and physical security implications. Address the trade-offs between convenience and security in embedded OTA systems, including recommendations for production environments with custom security measures. Include troubleshooting guidance for common web interface issues, browser compatibility considerations, and network connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Message Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed CAN message protocol documentation for ForwarderKE's J1939-like implementation. Document all custom message types including PF_HEARTBEAT for device status, PF_CONFIG_AXIS for axis configuration, PF_IDENTIFY for device identification, PF_SET_ADDRESS for address assignment, and PF_CAN_OUTPUT for GPIO control rules. Specify message format with PF (Priority/Parameter Group), SA (Source Address), DLC (Data Length Code), and data payload structure. Define parameter ranges, bit field layouts, and timing requirements for reliable communication. Include CAN ID formatting, arbitration mechanisms, and address claiming procedures. Document message sequencing, acknowledgment patterns, and error handling strategies. Provide examples of complete message frames with hex values and explain the significance of each data byte in the context of ForwarderKE operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Common Issues and Resolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for common ForwarderKE issues and their resolution strategies. Document frequent problems including CAN bus communication failures, address claiming conflicts during startup, solenoid control malfunctions, and web interface connectivity issues. Explain the root causes of each issue, step-by-step resolution procedures, and preventive measures to avoid recurrence. Cover specific scenarios such as address collision resolution, heartbeat timeout handling, bus-off recovery procedures, and I2C communication failures with PCA9685. Detail troubleshooting steps for WiFi OTA update failures, web server connectivity problems, and configuration corruption. Include error message interpretations, symptom analysis, and corrective actions for each common failure mode. Provide quick-reference solutions and escalation procedures for complex issues that require deeper investigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization and security documentation for ForwarderKE. Detail memory usage optimization techniques including heap management, stack allocation strategies, and efficient data structures for embedded environments. Document real-time performance improvements such as interrupt handling optimization, CAN bus timing considerations, and responsive web interface performance. Explain power consumption optimization for battery-powered agricultural equipment including sleep modes, duty cycling, and low-power CAN transceiver configurations. Address security considerations for production deployments including CAN bus message filtering, access control mechanisms, and protection against malicious traffic injection. Document secure configuration management, OTA update verification processes, and network security best practices. Include performance monitoring techniques, profiling tools, and benchmarking methodologies for embedded CAN systems. Provide guidance on optimizing the ESP32 resource utilization and managing concurrent operations in real-time embedded environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Button State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the button state management system within the Joystick ECU. Detail the digital input handling for joystick buttons, including pull-up/pull-down resistor configurations and interrupt-driven detection. Explain the debouncing algorithms used to eliminate contact bounce and ensure reliable button state detection. Document the state change detection mechanisms, including rising/falling edge detection and long press recognition. Cover the button mapping configuration, custom button assignments, and multi-button coordination. Include the button press timing characteristics, repeat functionality for sustained inputs, and button combination detection. Provide practical examples of button configuration, debouncing parameter tuning, and troubleshooting button responsiveness issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: PWM Mapping Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PWM mapping algorithm that converts joystick values to solenoid actuation signals. Detail the bidirectional axis handling with deadband calculation, forward and reverse mapping functions, and the mathematical transformation from 10-bit ADC values to 12-bit PWM values. Explain the deadband threshold calculation, linear interpolation within deadband regions, and the conversion formula ((value * 4095) / 255). Document the non-blocking axis processing loop, real-time value updates, and the relationship between joystick input channels and solenoid output channels. Include examples of axis configuration parameters, calibration procedures, and troubleshooting common mapping issues like incomplete travel or inconsistent response curves.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for configuration management API endpoints including /api/config for axis mapping configuration and /api/canoutput for GPIO rule configuration. Document the GET /api/config endpoint which returns current motor driver configuration including pcaCount and axes array with sourceAddress, potIndex, outputChannel, deadbandMin, deadbandMax, pwmMin, pwmMax, and flags for each axis. Document the POST /api/config endpoint for saving new axis mappings with the complete request payload structure. For /api/canoutput, document both GET endpoint returning current CAN output rules and POST endpoint for saving new rules. Include detailed JSON schemas for both request and response payloads, parameter validation rules, and practical examples of configuration operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Output Rules Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for CAN output rules configuration endpoints. Document the GET /api/canoutput endpoint for retrieving current CAN-triggered GPIO output rules including enabled flag, matchPF, matchSA, gpioPin, mode, and momentaryMs for each rule. Document the POST /api/canoutput endpoint for saving new CAN output rules with the request payload structure containing ruleIdx, enabled, matchPF, matchSA, gpioPin, mode, and momentaryMs for each rule. Include practical examples of rule configuration, validation rules for GPIO pin selection and timing parameters, and integration with external devices like relays or indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hardware Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive hardware architecture documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the physical system design and component interconnections. Document the ESP32-S3 LilyGO T-CAN board specifications, built-in CAN transceiver capabilities, and pin assignments for all peripherals including WS2812 status LED, I2C connections for PCA9685, and analog inputs for joystick potentiometers. Explain the PCA9685 PWM controller setup with 8 MOSFET outputs for solenoid control, including power distribution and load characteristics. Detail the joystick input circuitry with 3 potentiometers and 2 buttons per controller, including signal conditioning and debouncing circuits. Document the CAN bus topology, termination requirements, and signal integrity considerations. Include wiring diagrams, component selection guidelines, and power supply requirements. Address safety considerations for high-current solenoid loads and proper grounding techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: PWM Driver System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PCA9685 PWM driver system used for solenoid control. Document the I2C communication setup between ESP32 and PCA9685, including SDA/SCL pin assignments and clock frequency considerations. Explain the 8-channel MOSFET output configuration for controlling agricultural solenoids, including current ratings, switching frequencies, and load characteristics. Detail the PWM duty cycle scaling from 0-255 to 12-bit resolution and the corresponding solenoid actuation timing. Document the safety timeout mechanism that automatically shuts off all solenoids after 500ms of inactivity. Include wiring diagrams for solenoid connections, flyback diode protection circuits, and power distribution considerations. Address heat dissipation and thermal management for continuous solenoid operation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Library Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ForwarderKE's shared library design focusing on the separation of concerns between ForwarderCAN and ForwarderConfig libraries. Document the ForwarderCAN library's role in implementing J1939-like CAN protocol with address claiming and message handling. Explain the ForwarderConfig library's responsibility for persistent storage using NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) and configuration management. Detail the library interfaces, dependency relationships, and how they integrate with ECU implementations. Include the modular design benefits, code reusability patterns, and extension points for future enhancements. Address library initialization sequences, global state management, and inter-library communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Protocol Timing and Sequencing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for CAN protocol timing and message sequencing in ForwarderKE. Document the timing requirements for address claiming operations including CLAIM_TIMEOUT_MS (250ms) and CLAIM_RETRY_MS (1000ms) intervals. Explain the heartbeat message transmission patterns and timing intervals used for system health monitoring. Detail the message queuing and transmission priorities based on the priority field (bits 28-26). Document the timing considerations for joystick input reporting, solenoid command execution, and configuration message handling. Explain the relationship between message frequency and system responsiveness requirements. Include timing diagrams showing typical message sequences, arbitration delays, and recovery timeouts. Address timing constraints for real-time operation and potential timing conflicts between different ECU types. Provide guidance on optimizing timing parameters for different operational scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: ECU Type Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the ECU type selection mechanism that determines which ECU implementation is compiled into the firmware. Explain the compile-time configuration using ECU_TYPE_MOTOR_DRIVER and ECU_TYPE_JOYSTICK preprocessor directives. Document the PlatformIO environment configuration that enables different ECU types through build targets. Detail how the selection affects code inclusion, hardware pin assignments, and feature availability. Explain the conditional compilation approach that allows a single codebase to support multiple ECU variants. Cover the implications for hardware requirements, memory usage, and functionality differences between motor driver and joystick implementations. Include practical guidance on selecting the appropriate build environment and understanding the trade-offs between ECU types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Validation and Migration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for configuration validation and migration procedures in ForwarderKE's configuration management system. Detail the default configuration loading mechanism, validation rules for configuration parameters, and safe fallback procedures when configurations are invalid or missing. Document the configuration versioning system, schema evolution procedures, and backward compatibility maintenance. Explain validation checks for axis mappings, PWM ranges, CAN rule parameters, and address conflicts. Cover migration procedures for upgrading from older configuration versions, data transformation processes, and validation error reporting. Include configuration integrity verification, consistency checks across related settings, and automatic repair mechanisms. Provide examples of validation scenarios, migration workflows, and troubleshooting validation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the configuration management system focused on the axis mapping interface and device configuration controls. Document the axis configuration panel allowing 16-channel joystick-to-solenoid mapping with source selection, deadband adjustment, PWM range configuration, and bidirectional operation settings. Explain the configuration persistence mechanism, JSON-based configuration transfer, and real-time validation of parameter ranges. Detail the CAN output rule configuration interface for GPIO triggering with pattern matching, target address filtering, output modes (toggle/momentary), and pulse duration settings. Cover the configuration backup and restore procedures, default value management, and cross-ECU configuration synchronization. Include practical examples of typical configuration scenarios, troubleshooting misconfigured mappings, and optimizing joystick-to-solenoid relationships for different agricultural applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Update Procedures and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive update procedures and validation documentation for ForwarderKE OTA firmware updates. Document the complete firmware update workflow from file selection through installation completion, including file format requirements (.bin files), size limitations, and compatibility checks. Explain the Update library integration including chunked transfer handling, progress reporting, and memory management during transfers. Detail the validation procedures including checksum verification, firmware integrity checks, and rollback protection mechanisms. Document the update process flow including upload initiation, progress monitoring, installation phases, and system reboot procedures. Address the differences between standard and OTA build environments, including environment-specific compilation flags and deployment considerations. Include step-by-step procedures for successful updates, common failure scenarios and their causes, and preventive measures to ensure update reliability. Provide guidance on update scheduling, backup procedures, and recovery from failed updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time State API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive real-time state API documentation for ForwarderKE's WebSocket-like state update mechanism. Document the /api/state endpoint that provides continuous system monitoring data including joystick inputs, solenoid outputs, CAN bus statistics, device modules, and operational status. Explain the polling interval (200ms), data streaming patterns, and state synchronization mechanisms. Detail the JSON response structure with joystick data arrays, solenoid value mappings, module discovery information, and CAN bus metrics. Document the client-side JavaScript implementation showing how the web interface consumes real-time data through periodic fetch calls. Include examples of state object structures, data freshness indicators, and error recovery mechanisms. Address performance considerations for real-time monitoring and browser compatibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Safety and Maintenance Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive safety guidelines and maintenance practices documentation for ForwarderKE systems. Document safety protocols for working with CAN bus systems, electrical hazards, and proper equipment handling procedures. Explain the system's built-in safety features including address claiming arbitration, solenoid timeout protection, bus-off recovery, and heartbeat monitoring. Detail preventive maintenance schedules for field-deployed equipment including firmware update cycles, configuration backups, and hardware inspection procedures. Document proper shutdown procedures, power cycling protocols, and safe handling of CAN bus connections. Include guidelines for maintaining electrical isolation, protecting against ESD damage, and ensuring proper grounding. Address maintenance best practices for environmental factors, dust protection, and long-term storage procedures. Provide safety checklists for installation, operation, and decommissioning activities.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Integration and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed system integration and deployment documentation for ForwarderKE. Document advanced configuration scenarios including multi-machine coordination, centralized control systems, and integration with external monitoring platforms such as GPS systems, telemetry modules, and farm management software. Explain deployment strategies for large-scale agricultural operations with multiple tractors, implements, and control stations. Detail network topology considerations, CAN bus architecture planning, and signal integrity requirements for multi-ECU installations. Document integration patterns with existing agricultural equipment including OEM controller replacement, retrofit installations, and hybrid system operation. Address system monitoring and diagnostics including remote access capabilities, logging mechanisms, and fault detection systems. Include guidance for scaling the system to support additional ECU types, expanding functionality, and maintaining system reliability in harsh agricultural environments. Provide deployment checklists, commissioning procedures, and operational best practices for professional installations.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Message Broadcasting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the CAN message broadcasting functionality of the Joystick ECU. Document the PF_JOYSTICK_POT1/2/3 message types, including payload structure, data encoding, and transmission intervals. Explain the CAN message formatting process, priority scheduling, and arbitration avoidance techniques. Detail the heartbeat mechanism for maintaining connection status with motor driver ECUs and the timeout handling for connection loss detection. Cover the message rate configuration, burst transmission prevention, and bandwidth optimization strategies. Document the CAN bus arbitration process, collision detection, and retransmission handling. Include practical examples of CAN message monitoring, protocol debugging, and network performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Safety Timeout Mechanism
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the safety timeout mechanism that automatically shuts off solenoids after 500ms of inactivity. Explain the timeout detection logic using lastSolenoidUpdate timestamps, the allOff() function implementation that resets all solenoid values to zero, and the automatic deactivation process. Detail the timeout configuration constants, the timing precision considerations, and the relationship between joystick activity detection and solenoid power management. Cover the safety implications of automatic shutdown, the heartbeat monitoring system, and the prevention of solenoid overheating or continuous operation. Include practical examples of timeout behavior during normal operation, troubleshooting procedures for timeout-related issues, and safety guidelines for field operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for device management API endpoints including /api/identify for device identification and /api/address for dynamic address assignment. Document the POST /api/identify endpoint which sends identify commands to target devices using the request payload format with target parameter. Document the POST /api/address endpoint for changing device addresses dynamically with target and address parameters. Include practical examples of device discovery workflows, address claiming procedures, and error handling for invalid addresses or unavailable devices. Explain the relationship between these endpoints and the CAN heartbeat scanning system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Software Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive software architecture documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the modular embedded system design and build configuration management. Document the ECU type selection system using preprocessor directives and PlatformIO environment configuration, explaining how compile-time flags determine motor driver vs joystick functionality. Explain the modular design principles with separate libraries for CAN communication and configuration management. Detail the main.cpp entry point logic, ECU initialization sequence, and runtime component coordination. Document the PlatformIO build system with six distinct environments for different ECU types and deployment scenarios. Include the shared library architecture with ForwarderCAN and ForwarderConfig libraries, explaining their roles in protocol implementation and persistent storage. Address memory management, real-time constraints, and interrupt handling strategies. Provide system context diagrams showing component relationships and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Joystick Input Circuits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the joystick input circuitry including three potentiometer channels and two button inputs per controller. Document the analog input configuration using ESP32 ADC capabilities, including 10-bit resolution and reference voltage considerations. Explain the signal conditioning circuitry for potentiometer inputs, including filtering and noise reduction techniques. Detail the button input configuration with active-low logic and internal pull-up resistors. Document the debouncing algorithms implemented in software to handle mechanical switch bounce. Include calibration procedures for joystick zero positions and travel limits. Explain the CAN message transmission format for joystick data including the 10-bit value encoding and button state reporting. Address input sensitivity adjustment and deadband configuration for optimal operator feedback.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Initialization Sequence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete system initialization sequence in ForwarderKE from boot-up to operational state. Trace the execution flow from main.cpp setup() function through ECU-specific initialization routines. Detail the serial communication setup, hardware initialization order, and component registration processes. Explain the ECU-specific initialization functions (ecu_motor_driver_setup vs ecu_joystick_setup) and their unique requirements. Document the watchdog initialization, interrupt configuration, and real-time task scheduling. Include timing considerations, startup error handling, and diagnostic output patterns. Address the graceful degradation strategies and fallback mechanisms during initialization failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Fault Tolerance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for CAN error handling and fault tolerance mechanisms in ForwarderKE. Document the built-in error counting system with getErrorCount() method and error tracking mechanisms. Explain automatic recovery procedures for bus-off conditions and error passive states. Detail the watchdog timer implementation and timeout handling for address claiming operations. Document error detection and reporting mechanisms including frame errors, bit errors, and arbitration losses. Explain the fault tolerance strategies for message retransmission and degraded operation modes. Detail the error statistics collection including TX/RX error counts and their significance for system diagnostics. Include troubleshooting procedures for common CAN bus issues such as arbitration conflicts, message corruption, and communication timeouts. Provide guidance on interpreting error codes and implementing custom error handling routines. Address safety considerations and graceful degradation when CAN communication fails.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Interface Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the web interface integration with ForwarderKE's configuration management system. Explain how configuration data flows between the NVS storage, web server, and real-time state management. Detail the HTTP API endpoints for configuration retrieval and modification, JSON data structures for configuration transfer, and real-time synchronization mechanisms. Document the web_state integration for sharing configuration data across different system components, state synchronization protocols, and configuration change propagation. Cover the user interface for configuration management including form validation, real-time feedback, and error display. Include examples of configuration modification workflows, web API usage patterns, and troubleshooting web interface configuration issues. Address security considerations for configuration access and modification.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTA Firmware Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Over-The-Air firmware update system focused on the web-based update interface and secure firmware deployment. Document the firmware upload process using multipart file uploads, progress tracking with visual indicators, and update validation procedures. Explain the Update library integration, memory allocation for firmware storage, and safe update execution with automatic device restart. Detail the update security considerations, file format requirements (.bin files), and rollback protection mechanisms. Cover the update workflow from file selection through upload completion, error handling for interrupted transfers, and post-update verification. Include practical examples of the complete update process, troubleshooting common update failures, and best practices for field deployments. Address the WiFi access point mode for initial device setup and the mDNS hostname resolution for device discovery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and Recovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive troubleshooting and recovery documentation for ForwarderKE OTA firmware updates. Document common failure scenarios including network connectivity issues, insufficient storage space, corrupted firmware files, and interrupted transfers. Explain diagnostic procedures for identifying the root cause of update failures, including serial monitor output interpretation, LED status indicators, and web interface error messages. Detail recovery mechanisms including manual firmware recovery through serial programming, safe mode operation, and factory reset procedures. Document rollback procedures for reverting to previous firmware versions, including the importance of maintaining backup copies and version management. Address field-deployed maintenance procedures including remote diagnostics, update scheduling for multiple units, and emergency recovery protocols. Include preventive maintenance practices such as regular firmware updates, storage optimization, and environmental considerations for reliable OTA operations. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting guides for common scenarios and escalation procedures for complex issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Interface Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed web interface integration documentation for ForwarderKE's embedded web server. Document the complete HTML/CSS/JavaScript implementation including tabbed interface design, real-time dashboard rendering, form validation, and user interaction patterns. Explain the client-side JavaScript architecture with functions for state fetching, configuration management, and real-time updates. Detail the grid-based layout system for responsive design, slider controls for configuration parameters, and progress indicators for OTA updates. Document the integration patterns between frontend and backend APIs, error handling strategies, and user feedback mechanisms. Include examples of DOM manipulation, event handling, and AJAX request patterns used throughout the interface. Address browser compatibility, mobile responsiveness, and accessibility considerations for the web-based configuration and monitoring interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Recovery Procedures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed system recovery procedures documentation for ForwarderKE covering recovery from various failure scenarios and system restoration methods. Document recovery procedures for system crashes, data corruption, configuration loss, and hardware faults. Explain the rollback procedures for failed firmware updates, recovery from CAN bus arbitration failures, and restoration of default configurations. Detail the process for recovering from address claiming conflicts, resolving stuck solenoid states, and restoring normal operation after timeout conditions. Document backup and restore procedures for configuration data, firmware recovery using OTA methods, and emergency system reset procedures. Include step-by-step recovery workflows for complex failure scenarios, escalation procedures for unrecoverable faults, and procedures for returning systems to operational status. Address recovery from power loss situations, CAN bus off conditions, and web interface failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Practices and Contributing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development practices and contribution documentation for ForwarderKE. Document code organization principles including modular design patterns, header file management, and cross-platform compatibility considerations for ESP32 development. Explain the development workflow using PlatformIO including environment configuration, multi-target builds, and continuous integration processes. Detail coding standards, naming conventions, and documentation requirements for contributors. Document the testing methodology including unit testing strategies, integration testing approaches, and validation procedures for embedded CAN systems. Address version control practices, branch management, and release procedures using GitHub workflows. Include guidance for contributing to the open-source project including issue reporting, feature requests, pull request submission, and code review processes. Document the build system architecture, dependency management, and cross-compilation considerations. Provide templates and examples for developing new ECU types, extending functionality, and maintaining backward compatibility. Include best practices for embedded systems development, debugging techniques, and performance analysis in resource-constrained environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Data Transmission
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the real-time data transmission system within the Joystick ECU. Detail the timing constraints for joystick data sampling, including minimum sampling intervals and maximum latency requirements. Explain the interrupt-driven data collection system, priority scheduling, and context switching considerations. Document the data buffering mechanisms, circular buffers, and memory management for continuous data streaming. Cover the transmission rate optimization, data compression techniques, and bandwidth utilization strategies. Detail the fault tolerance mechanisms, data integrity checks, and error recovery procedures. Include practical examples of timing analysis, buffer management, and performance monitoring for real-time operation.

wiki_catalogs.name: LED Status Indicator System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the NeoPixel LED status indicator system. Document the RGB color management, blinking patterns for different operational states, and the identification mode functionality. Explain the LED state machine including connection status indicators (solid blue for normal, blinking red for offline), fast blink warnings (yellow for active communication), and identification mode (blinking white for 3 seconds). Detail the update timing logic with 50ms refresh intervals, color blending calculations, and the integration with the main control loop. Cover the LED pin configuration, NeoPixelBus library setup, and troubleshooting procedures for LED display issues. Include examples of LED behavior during different system states and diagnostic procedures using LED patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firmware Update Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the firmware update endpoint /update. Document both POST /update endpoint for initiating OTA firmware updates and POST /update handler for receiving firmware data chunks. Explain the multipart form-data upload process, progress tracking through HTTPUpload callbacks, and the complete firmware update workflow from file selection to device reboot. Include practical examples of curl commands for firmware uploads, JavaScript fetch API patterns for web-based updates, and error handling scenarios including network failures and invalid firmware files. Document security considerations, update verification processes, and rollback procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Protocol Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CAN protocol documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the J1939-like addressing scheme and custom message implementation. Document the 29-bit extended ID format with priority, data page, parameter group number, and source addressing fields. Explain the address claiming and arbitration mechanism used during system startup to prevent conflicts between multiple ECUs. Detail all defined CAN message types including joystick position reporting, button state transmission, LED color control, solenoid command messages, and heartbeat/status broadcasts. Document the message payload formats, data encoding schemes, and timing requirements. Include protocol timing diagrams, message sequence charts, and error handling procedures. Explain the broadcast vs directed message patterns and their respective use cases. Address CAN bus error handling, automatic recovery mechanisms, and fault tolerance features. Provide practical examples of message construction and interpretation using the actual codebase implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the global state management system focused on cross-ECU data sharing and real-time state synchronization. Document the global state variables structure including joystick data arrays, solenoid value tracking, module discovery information, and CAN bus statistics counters. Explain the state update mechanisms from different ECU types, data serialization for web API responses, and state consistency across multiple components. Detail the heartbeat scanning system for module detection, state aging mechanisms for stale data handling, and real-time data aggregation from CAN messages. Cover the state persistence requirements, memory management for large state arrays, and thread-safe access patterns. Include practical examples of state data structures, API response formatting, and debugging state synchronization issues. Address the relationship between global state and individual ECU implementations, state initialization sequences, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Heartbeat Message Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the heartbeat message generation system. Detail the 1-second periodic transmission containing system health metrics including CAN bus online status, uptime in seconds, receive/transmit message counts, channel count information, and PCA9685 controller presence. Explain the PF_HEARTBEAT message format, data payload structure, and the integration with the ForwarderCAN messaging system. Document the timestamp handling, counter management, and the broadcast nature of heartbeat messages. Cover the monitoring capabilities for system diagnostics, the relationship between heartbeat frequency and system responsiveness, and troubleshooting procedures using heartbeat data. Include examples of heartbeat message contents, monitoring tools, and interpretation of heartbeat statistics for system health assessment.

wiki_catalogs.name: ECU Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive ECU implementation documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the two distinct ECU types and their specialized functions. Document the Motor Driver ECU implementation including PCA9685 PWM control logic, solenoid valve actuation sequences, and safety timeout mechanisms. Explain the joystick ECU implementation covering analog input processing, button state management, input filtering and calibration procedures, and CAN message broadcasting. Detail the compile-time ECU type selection mechanism and how it affects code compilation and functionality. Include the shared CAN communication layer usage, configuration dependency management, and state synchronization between ECUs. Document the real-time constraints, interrupt handling, and timing requirements for both ECU types. Address the differences in hardware interfaces, processing requirements, and safety considerations between motor control and input acquisition roles. Provide practical examples of ECU-specific configuration and troubleshooting procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Address Claiming and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the address claiming process and configuration management. Document the ForwarderConfig integration for persistent storage, the getForcedAddress() function for manual address assignment, and the address validation process (0x20-0xEF range). Explain the configuration loading sequence, the loadMotorConfig() function, and the relationship between stored settings and runtime behavior. Detail the CAN message processing for PF_SET_ADDRESS commands, the address change procedure with automatic reboot, and the configuration persistence system. Cover the ECU_NAME identification bytes, the preferred address constants, and the configuration backup and restore procedures. Include practical examples of address assignment, configuration modification via CAN commands, and troubleshooting address conflicts or configuration corruption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration management documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the persistent storage system and device settings management. Document the NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) implementation for storing device configurations, axis mappings, and operational parameters. Explain the configuration data structures including AxisConfig for joystick-to-solenoid mapping, MotorConfig for driver settings, and CanOutputRule for GPIO control rules. Detail the default configuration loading, user-modifiable settings, and configuration persistence across reboots. Document the configuration validation procedures, safe default fallback mechanisms, and configuration backup/restore capabilities. Include the web interface integration for configuration management, real-time parameter updates, and configuration synchronization between ECUs. Address configuration versioning, backward compatibility, and migration procedures for configuration schema changes. Provide practical examples of configuration modification, validation testing, and troubleshooting configuration-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive web interface documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the real-time monitoring dashboard and configuration management system. Document the HTTP server implementation, REST API endpoints for device control and monitoring, and WebSocket-like real-time state updates. Explain the dashboard interface showing CAN bus statistics, ECU status, joystick positions, and solenoid states. Detail the configuration management features including device address assignment, axis mapping adjustments, and operational parameter tuning. Document the OTA firmware update interface, WiFi access point functionality, and remote device management capabilities. Include the global state management system, cross-ECU data sharing mechanisms, and real-time data synchronization. Address the web interface security considerations, access control mechanisms, and network connectivity requirements. Provide practical examples of web interface usage, API endpoint testing, and troubleshooting web-based operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAN Output Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CAN output rules documentation for ForwarderKE focused on GPIO output control and external device integration capabilities. Document the rule-based system for triggering GPIO outputs based on CAN message conditions, including toggle and momentary operation modes. Explain the CanOutputRule data structure, rule configuration parameters, and persistence mechanisms. Detail the integration with external devices such as indicator lights, alarms, or additional control systems through GPIO outputs. Document the rule evaluation logic, timing considerations, and safety mechanisms to prevent conflicting output states. Include practical examples of rule configuration for common scenarios like emergency stop indicators, operational status displays, and diagnostic signaling. Address the relationship between CAN message processing and GPIO output activation, including filtering and debouncing of CAN-triggered events. Provide troubleshooting guidance for rule-based output problems and integration testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTA Firmware Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive OTA firmware update documentation for ForwarderKE focused on the wireless firmware upgrade system and deployment procedures. Document the WiFi access point creation during OTA mode, web-based firmware upload interface, and automatic firmware installation process. Explain the security considerations for OTA updates including access point credentials, update validation procedures, and rollback mechanisms. Detail the firmware.bin generation process, update file preparation, and deployment workflows for different ECU types. Document the update process flow from web interface interaction through firmware installation and system reboot. Include troubleshooting procedures for failed updates, recovery mechanisms for corrupted firmware, and rollback to previous versions. Address the differences between standard and OTA build environments, update scheduling considerations, and field-deployed maintenance procedures. Provide practical examples of successful update procedures, common failure scenarios, and preventive maintenance practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for ForwarderKE focused on all public interfaces and communication protocols. Document the HTTP REST API endpoints including GET/POST methods for device configuration, status queries, and control commands. Detail the CAN message protocol specification with complete message format definitions, parameter ranges, and timing requirements. Explain the WebSocket-like real-time state update interface, event types, and data streaming protocols. Document the web interface API for dashboard integration, configuration management endpoints, and OTA update interfaces. Include complete request/response schemas, authentication methods, error codes, and status indicators. Provide practical examples of API usage with curl commands, JavaScript client implementations, and integration patterns. Address API versioning, backward compatibility considerations, and deprecation procedures. Include rate limiting, security considerations, and performance optimization guidelines for API consumers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and maintenance documentation for ForwarderKE focused on diagnostic procedures, problem resolution, and system maintenance practices. Document common issues including CAN bus communication failures, address claiming conflicts, solenoid control problems, and web interface connectivity issues. Explain diagnostic procedures using CAN bus analyzers, oscilloscope measurements, and serial debug output interpretation. Detail the system's built-in diagnostic capabilities including heartbeat monitoring, error counters, and status reporting. Document maintenance procedures for field-deployed equipment including firmware update cycles, configuration backups, and preventive maintenance schedules. Include safety guidelines for working with CAN bus systems, electrical hazards, and proper equipment handling. Address performance tuning procedures, optimization techniques, and system monitoring best practices. Provide systematic troubleshooting workflows, diagnostic checklists, and escalation procedures for complex issues. Include recovery procedures for system failures, data corruption, and hardware faults.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive advanced topics documentation for ForwarderKE focused on protocol extensions, custom development, and system optimization. Document potential protocol enhancements including additional message types, extended addressing schemes, and custom parameter group numbers. Explain the process for developing custom ECU types, extending the existing CAN protocol, and integrating new hardware components. Detail performance optimization techniques including memory usage reduction, real-time performance improvements, and power consumption optimization. Address security considerations for production deployments, access control mechanisms, and protection against malicious CAN traffic. Document advanced configuration scenarios including multi-machine coordination, centralized control systems, and integration with external monitoring platforms. Include reverse engineering techniques for understanding existing CAN traffic, protocol analysis tools, and system integration patterns. Provide guidance for contributing to the open-source project, code organization principles, and development best practices for embedded CAN systems.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ForwarderKE: CAN-Based Agricultural Vehicle Control System

## Project Introduction

ForwarderKE is an innovative CAN (Controller Area Network) communication system designed for agricultural vehicle control applications. The project provides a modular, real-time control solution that enables precise valve actuation and joystick-based control through an ESP32-based embedded system architecture.

### Purpose Statement
The system serves as a bridge between human operators and agricultural machinery, translating joystick inputs into precise solenoid valve commands for automated field operations. It implements a custom J1939-like protocol over CAN bus to create a robust, industrial-grade control network.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Control**: Provide millisecond-level response times for critical agricultural operations
- **Modular Architecture**: Support multiple ECU types (motor drivers and joysticks) in a unified network
- **Remote Configuration**: Enable dynamic reconfiguration through web-based interfaces
- **Industrial Reliability**: Implement watchdog timers, safety timeouts, and fault detection mechanisms
- **OTA Updates**: Support wireless firmware updates for field-deployed equipment

### Target Audience
- Agricultural equipment manufacturers
- Farm automation system integrators
- Research institutions developing precision agriculture solutions
- DIY enthusiasts building custom agricultural control systems

## Technical Architecture

The ForwarderKE system follows a distributed embedded architecture with clear separation of concerns across hardware abstraction, protocol handling, and application logic layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[ESP32 Microcontroller] --> B[Hardware Abstraction Layer]
    B --> C[CAN Protocol Layer]
    B --> D[Application Layer]
    
    C --> E[ForwarderCAN Library]
    C --> F[Custom J1939 Protocol]
    
    D --> G[Motor Driver ECU]
    D --> H[Joystick ECU]
    D --> I[Configuration Manager]
    
    G --> J[PCA9685 PWM Controllers]
    G --> K[Neopixel LED Indicators]
    
    H --> L[Analog Inputs]
    H --> M[Digital Buttons]
    
    I --> N[NVS Storage]
    I --> O[Preferences Management]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Hardware Layer**: ESP32 microcontrollers with integrated CAN transceivers, providing the foundation for real-time communication and control.

**Protocol Layer**: Custom implementation of J1939-like addressing scheme with 29-bit ID structure supporting priority, destination addressing, and payload identification.

**Application Layer**: Two distinct ECU types with specialized functionality for different roles in the agricultural control ecosystem.

### Design Patterns

The system employs several key design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern**: Environment-specific builds through PlatformIO configuration
- **Strategy Pattern**: Dual ECU type support (motor driver vs joystick)
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time state monitoring and web interface updates
- **Command Pattern**: CAN message processing and action execution

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant J as Joystick ECU
    participant CAN as CAN Bus
    participant M as Motor Driver ECU
    participant S as Solenoids
    
    J->>+CAN: Potentiometer Data (PF=0x10-0x12)
    CAN->>+M: Forwarded Control Signals
    M->>+S: PWM Commands (PF=0x21)
    S-->>-M: Valve Response
    M->>+CAN: Heartbeat Status
    CAN-->>-J: Network Health
    
    Note over J,M: Bidirectional Communication
    Note over CAN: Real-time Control Loop
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The system initialization follows a standardized pattern across all ECU types:

**Primary Entry Point**: `src/main.cpp` - Centralized application startup with ECU-type selection through preprocessor directives

**ECU-Specific Implementations**:
- `src/ecu_motor_driver.cpp` - Valve control and PWM management
- `src/ecu_joystick.cpp` - Input acquisition and signal transmission

### Core Modules

**CAN Communication Layer**: `lib/ForwarderCAN/ForwarderCAN.h` - Implements custom J1939-like protocol with 29-bit addressing and priority handling

**Configuration Management**: `lib/ForwarderConfig/ForwarderConfig.h` - Persistent storage for axis mappings, device addresses, and CAN output rules

**Web Interface**: `src/ota_webserver.cpp` - Integrated web server for remote monitoring and configuration

**CAN Output Rules**: `src/can_output.cpp` - GPIO output control based on incoming CAN messages

### Configuration Approach

**PlatformIO Configuration**: `platformio.ini` - Multi-environment build system supporting:
- Motor driver configurations (standard and OTA-enabled variants)
- Joystick configurations (two independent units)
- Hardware pin mapping and feature flags

**Build Flags**: Environment-specific compilation constants defining:
- Hardware pin assignments
- Device addresses and names  
- Feature enable/disable switches
- Safety and timing parameters

### External Dependencies

**Core Libraries**:
- `Adafruit_PWM_Servo_Driver` - PCA9685 PWM controller support
- `NeoPixelBus` - RGB LED control for status indication
- `Arduino` - ESP32 development framework

**Development Tools**:
- `PlatformIO IDE` - Cross-platform build system
- `GitHub Actions` - Automated CI/CD pipeline

### Integration Points

**Hardware Integration**:
- CAN transceiver connections (TX/RX/SE pins)
- I2C bus for PCA9685 controllers
- Analog inputs for potentiometer readings
- Digital inputs for button states

**Network Integration**:
- J1939-compatible addressing scheme
- Broadcast messaging for system-wide commands
- Point-to-point communication for device-specific operations

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class ForwarderCAN {
        +begin(txPin, rxPin, bitrate)
        +send(pf, ps, data, len, priority)
        +receive(msg, timeout)
        +isOnline() bool
        +getAddress() uint8_t
    }
    
    class ForwarderConfig {
        +getForcedAddress(defaultAddr) uint8_t
        +saveAxisConfig(axisIdx, axis)
        +saveCanOutputRule(index, rule)
        +loadDefaults(cfg)
    }
    
    class MotorDriverECU {
        +ecu_setup()
        +ecu_loop()
        -processCAN()
        -updateAxes()
        -initPCA()
    }
    
    class JoystickECU {
        +ecu_setup()
        +ecu_loop()
        -readInputs()
        -sendPot(pf, value)
        -sendButtons()
    }
    
    ForwarderCAN <|-- MotorDriverECU
    ForwarderCAN <|-- JoystickECU
    ForwarderConfig --> MotorDriverECU
    ForwarderConfig --> JoystickECU
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Control System**: The motor driver ECU processes joystick inputs and translates them into precise PWM signals for solenoid valve control with sub-millisecond response times.

**Dual Joystick Support**: Independent operation of two joystick units (0x21 and 0x22) enabling multi-operator agricultural machinery control.

**Dynamic Configuration**: Runtime adjustment of joystick-to-solenoid mappings through web interface with persistent storage capabilities.

**Safety Mechanisms**: Comprehensive safety features including watchdog timers, safety timeouts, and automatic emergency shutdown procedures.

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced PWM Control**: `src/ecu_motor_driver.cpp` - Sophisticated axis mapping with deadband compensation and bidirectional control support

**Web-based Monitoring**: `src/ota_webserver.cpp` - Real-time dashboard showing joystick positions, solenoid states, and network health metrics

**Remote Firmware Updates**: `src/ota_webserver.cpp` - Over-the-air firmware updates with progress tracking and rollback capabilities

**CAN Output Rules**: `src/can_output.cpp` - Configurable GPIO output triggers based on incoming CAN messages for external device control

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialization
    Initialization --> AddressClaiming: CAN Init Success
    Initialization --> ErrorState: CAN Init Failed
    
    AddressClaiming --> Online: Address Claimed
    AddressClaiming --> Retry: Claim Failed
    
    Retry --> AddressClaiming: Retry Timer
    Online --> Operational: All Systems Go
    Online --> Offline: Bus Failure
    
    Operational --> Configuration: Web Request
    Configuration --> Operational: Settings Saved
    
    Offline --> AddressClaiming: Bus Restored
    ErrorState --> [*]: Manual Reset
    
    Operational --> [*]: Shutdown Request
```

The ForwarderKE system represents a sophisticated integration of embedded control, network communication, and web-based management for modern agricultural automation. Its modular architecture and comprehensive feature set make it suitable for a wide range of precision agriculture applications while maintaining the reliability and safety standards required for industrial equipment.

**Sources:**
- [platformio.ini](platformio.ini)
- [main.cpp](src/main.cpp)
- [ecu_motor_driver.cpp](src/ecu_motor_driver.cpp)
- [ecu_joystick.cpp](src/ecu_joystick.cpp)
- [ForwarderCAN.h](lib/ForwarderCAN/ForwarderCAN.h)
- [ForwarderConfig.h](lib/ForwarderConfig/ForwarderConfig.h)
- [ota_webserver.cpp](src/ota_webserver.cpp)
- [can_output.cpp](src/can_output.cpp)
- [web_state.cpp](src/web_state.cpp)
- [build.yml](.github/workflows/build.yml)

</blog>
